label: Confluence
description: Read, search, and manage Confluence pages and spaces from your Atlassian Cloud or Server instance.

form.title: Connect Confluence
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to read and manage your Confluence pages and spaces.

methods.label: API Token
methods.description: Paste an Atlassian API token with your login email and subdomain. The fastest path — no OAuth client setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with an Atlassian API Token

Atlassian API tokens act as per-user passwords scoped to the Atlassian account. No admin approval needed — any Confluence user can generate one.

### Steps

1. Log in to your Atlassian account and open [API token management](https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens).
2. Click **Create API token**.
3. Give it a name like `Anton` and click **Create**.
4. Copy the token shown (it will not be displayed again).
5. Paste the token into **API Token** below.
6. Enter the **email address** you use to log in to Atlassian.
7. Enter your **Atlassian subdomain** — the part before `.atlassian.net` in your Confluence URL (e.g. `mycompany` for `mycompany.atlassian.net`).

### Notes

- The token authenticates as you, so Anton can access everything your account can in Confluence.
- If you lose the token, generate a new one from the same page and update it in Anton.
- For Confluence Server or Data Center, use the **Personal Access Token** method instead — API tokens are Atlassian Cloud only.

fields.name: subdomain
fields.label: Atlassian Subdomain
fields.description: The part before .atlassian.net in your Confluence URL (e.g. mycompany for mycompany.atlassian.net).

fields.name: email
fields.label: Login Email
fields.description: The email address associated with your Atlassian account.

fields.name: api_token
fields.label: API Token
fields.description: Generated at id.atlassian.com → Security → API tokens. Shown only once — copy it immediately.

methods.label: Personal Access Token (Server / Data Center)
methods.description: For Confluence Server or Data Center instances hosted on your own infrastructure. Generate a PAT from your Confluence profile and enter your base URL.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Personal Access Token (Server / Data Center)

Confluence Server 7.9+ and Data Center support Personal Access Tokens (PATs) — long-lived tokens tied to your user account.

### Steps

1. Log in to your self-hosted Confluence instance.
2. Click your **profile avatar** (top right) → **Profile**.
3. Go to **Settings → Personal Access Tokens → Create token**.
4. Give the token a name like `Anton`, set an optional expiry, and click **Create**.
5. Copy the token shown — it will not be shown again.
6. Enter your Confluence **Base URL** (e.g. `https://confluence.mycompany.com`).
7. Enter your Confluence **username** (not an email — this is your Confluence login name).
8. Paste the PAT into **Personal Access Token** below.

### Notes

- PATs are tied to your user account and expire if you set an expiry during creation.
- Ensure your Confluence instance is accessible from the machine running Anton (VPN may be required).
- This method does not work for Atlassian Cloud — use **API Token** instead.

fields.name: base_url
fields.label: Confluence Base URL
fields.description: The root URL of your self-hosted Confluence instance, without a trailing slash.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Your Confluence login username (not an email address).

fields.name: personal_access_token
fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.description: Generated in Confluence → Profile → Settings → Personal Access Tokens. Shown only once.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Atlassian Cloud)
methods.description: Use an Atlassian OAuth 2.0 app to grant Anton access without sharing credentials. Requires creating an OAuth app in the Atlassian Developer Console.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with OAuth 2.0 (Atlassian Cloud)

This method uses an Atlassian OAuth 2.0 (3LO) app from the Atlassian Developer Console. More setup than an API token, but credentials are never shared directly.

### Steps

1. Go to [developer.atlassian.com](https://developer.atlassian.com/console/myapps/) and click **Create → OAuth 2.0 integration**.
2. Give the app a name like `Anton` and accept the terms.
3. Under **Permissions**, add **Confluence API** and enable these scopes:
   - `read:confluence-content.all`
   - `write:confluence-content`
   - `read:confluence-space.summary`
   - `read:confluence-user`
4. Under **Authorization**, add a callback URL. For Anton use `http://localhost:3000/oauth/callback` (or the URL Anton instructs you to use).
5. Go to **Settings** and copy the **Client ID** and **Secret**.
6. Paste both into the fields below, then click **Submit**.
7. A browser window will open — log in to Atlassian and grant access.

### Notes

- `offline_access` scope is requested automatically to issue a refresh token.
- The app must be in **Production** (or have your account as a developer) to work properly.
- Atlassian Cloud only — Server/Data Center does not support this OAuth flow.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From your Atlassian Developer Console app → Settings → Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID — found in the same Settings page of your Atlassian OAuth app.
